incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on first attempt

steps.text: Check the question. How many groups do we need?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count on second attempt

steps.text: I asked for 4 groups. That means 4 containers. Use the control to set it to 4.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong container count three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting container count to 4. Four empty containers appear one by one.

steps.text: Here's how it works. I need 4 groups, so I set the control to 4. That gives me 4 containers, one for each group. The number of groups is always how many containers you see.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the question again. How many in each group?

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container on second attempt

steps.text: I asked for 3 in each group. That means 3 items go in every container. Use the control to set it to 3.

incorrects.description: Student set wrong items per container three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting items per container to 3. Three items appear in each of the 4 containers, one at a time.

steps.text: Watch how I do this. I need 3 in each group, so I set the control to 3. Now every container gets 3 items. That makes 4 groups of 3, which we write as 4 times 3.
